preamble: The transformation from a voiceless palato-alveolar fricative to an alveolar flap is a type of rhotacism or lenition, where a sibilant weakens into a rhotic liquid, typically occurring in intervocalic or coda positions.

examples.note: Historical shifts in certain dialects where sibilants were reinterpreted or weakened into rhotics.
In some Brazilian dialects, the coda sibilant (which is phonetically [ʃ] in many varieties) can rhotacize in certain lexical items or rapid speech.

sources: Masica, C. P. (1991). The Indo-Aryan Languages.
Coutinho, I. L. (1976). Pontos de gramática histórica.
